title: Smart energy hubs could save fleets £18,000
snippet:
Co-siting solar panels, batteries and bi-directional charging could save money and cut emissions

The post Smart energy hubs could save fleets £18,000 appeared first on
ai_summary: Article highlights co-location of solar, batteries, and EV charging as a cost-saving and emissions-reduction opportunity for fleet operators, relevant to tem's I&C customer base and renewable energy deployment trends.
ai_reasoning: Background reading on market trends and customer value propositions. Demonstrates growing interest in integrated renewable solutions among commercial customers, but no specific competitor move, regulatory change, or direct business trigger.
